name: jai-lsp-vscode
description: Language Server Protocol client for the Jai programming language.

jai-lsp.serverPath.description: Absolute path to the jai-lsp binary. If empty, the extension uses the platform binary bundled in bin/ (e.g. bin/jai-lsp-darwin-arm64); if no bundled binary exists for your platform, it falls back to `jai-lsp` on PATH.

jai-lsp.compilerPath.description: Absolute path to the Jai compiler binary (e.g. /Users/you/Development/Tools/jai/bin/jai-macos). Used by the diagnostics subprocess. If unset, the server tries `jai` on PATH — which won't pick up shell aliases.

jai-lsp.inlayHints.enabled.description: Whether jai-lsp emits inlay hints (inferred-type annotations like `: s64` after `x :=`). Disable if you find them noisy. Note: VSCode also has a per-language `editor.inlayHints.enabled` setting that works without reloading.

jai-lsp.entryFile.description: Absolute path to your project's entry file (the one with `main :: ()`). When set, the type-check pass compiles THIS file regardless of which file you have open, so cross-module hovers and goto-type-definition work. If unset, jai-lsp tries to auto-detect by finding any file in the workspace that declares `main :: ()`. If THAT fails, it falls back to compiling whatever file you have open (which may miss imports declared in sibling files).

jai-lsp.trace.server.description: Trace LSP messages to/from the server.
